#!/bin/bash
# common.sh - Shared library for Sidekick hooks system
#
# This file contains all shared functions used by Sidekick handlers and features.
# Functions are organized into namespaces for clarity:
#   - LOGGING: Session-specific logging with levels
#   - CONFIGURATION: Config cascade and feature toggles
#   - PATH RESOLUTION: Scope detection and path management
#   - JSON PROCESSING: JSON parsing and extraction
#   - PROCESS MANAGEMENT: Background process lifecycle
#   - CLAUDE INVOCATION: LLM invocation with isolation
#   - WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT: Temporary workspace creation
#   - UTILITIES: General-purpose helpers
#
# This file is sourced once per hook invocation and should never spawn
# subprocesses (except intentional background processes via process_launch_background).

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED=1

# Strict mode
set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# ANSI COLOR CONSTANTS
# ============================================================================
readonly COLOR_RESET='\033[0m'
readonly COLOR_RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly COLOR_GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly COLOR_YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly COLOR_BLUE='\033[0;34m'
readonly COLOR_MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
readonly COLOR_CYAN='\033[0;36m'
readonly COLOR_GRAY='\033[0;90m'
readonly COLOR_DIM='\033[2m'
readonly COLOR_BOLD='\033[1m'

# ============================================================================
# ERROR TRAP
# ============================================================================
error_trap() {
    local line_number="$1"
    local exit_code="$?"
    echo -e "${COLOR_RED}ERROR: Failed at line ${line_number} with exit code ${exit_code}${COLOR_RESET}" >&2
}
trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL STATE
# ============================================================================
# Session-specific log file (set by log_init)
_LOG_FILE=""

# Global log file (always available)
_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""

# Sidekick root directory (set by path_get_sidekick_root)
# Preserve pre-set value (e.g., from tests) while initializing if unset
_SIDEKICK_ROOT="${_SIDEKICK_ROOT:-}"

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Initialize logging with dual log support (global + session-specific)
# Sets up:
#   - Global log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sidekick.log (always created)
#   - Session log: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/sidekick.log (only if session dir exists)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format, optional - if empty only global log is used)
# Globals:
#   _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE - set to global log file path
#   _LOG_FILE - set to session log file path (empty if session dir doesn't exist)
log_init() {
    local session_id="${1:-}"

    # Initialize global log file (always create .sidekick directory)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local sidekick_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick"
        mkdir -p "$sidekick_dir"
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE="${sidekick_dir}/sidekick.log"

        # Create global log file if it doesn't exist
        if [ ! -f "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
            touch "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
        fi
    else
        _GLOBAL_LOG_FILE=""
    fi

    # Initialize session-specific log file (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$session_id" ] && [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local session_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"

        # Check if session directory exists (don't create it)
        if [ -d "$session_dir" ]; then
            _LOG_FILE="${session_dir}/sidekick.log"

            # Create session log file if it doesn't exist
            if [ ! -f "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
                touch "$_LOG_FILE"
            fi
        else
            _LOG_FILE=""
        fi
    else
        _LOG_FILE=""
    fi
}

# Log debug message (only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug)
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_debug() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [ "$level" = "debug" ]; then
        _log_format_ansi "DEBUG" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "DEBUG" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log info message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_info() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "INFO" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "INFO" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log warning message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_warn() {
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn)$ ]]; then
        _log_format_ansi "WARN" "$*" >&2
        _log_to_file "WARN" "$*"
    fi
}

# Log error message
# Args:
#   $* - message to log
log_error() {
    _log_format_ansi "ERROR" "$*" >&2
    _log_to_file "ERROR" "$*"
}

# Internal: Write log entry to file(s)
# Writes to both global and session-specific logs (if available)
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_to_file() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    local log_line="[${timestamp}] [${level}] [PID:$$] ${message}"

    # Write to global log (always, if available)
    if [ -n "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_GLOBAL_LOG_FILE"
    fi

    # Write to session-specific log (only if session dir exists)
    if [ -n "$_LOG_FILE" ]; then
        echo "$log_line" >> "$_LOG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Internal: Format log message with ANSI colors
# Args:
#   $1 - log level
#   $2 - message
_log_format_ansi() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local color=""

    case "$level" in
        DEBUG) color="$COLOR_GRAY" ;;
        INFO)  color="$COLOR_GREEN" ;;
        WARN)  color="$COLOR_YELLOW" ;;
        ERROR) color="$COLOR_RED" ;;
        *)     color="$COLOR_RESET" ;;
    esac

    echo -e "${color}[${level}]${COLOR_RESET} ${message}"
}

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Load configuration cascade: defaults → user → project
# Sources config files in order, later values override earlier ones
# Globals:
#   All config variables (FEATURE_*, TOPIC_*, SLEEPER_*, etc.)
config_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    # 1. Load defaults (must exist)
    local defaults="${sidekick_root}/config.defaults"
    if [ ! -f "$defaults" ]; then
        echo "ERROR: config.defaults not found at ${defaults}" >&2
        exit 2
    fi
    # shellcheck disable=SC1090
    source "$defaults"

    # 2. Load user config (optional)
    # Allow override for testing (e.g., SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT=sidekick-test)
    local user_dir="${SIDEKICK_USER_ROOT:-sidekick}"
    local user_config="${HOME}/.claude/hooks/${user_dir}/sidekick.conf"
    if [ -f "$user_config" ]; then
        # shellcheck disable=SC1090
        source "$user_config"
    fi

    # 3. Load project config (optional)
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        local project_config="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
        if [ -f "$project_config" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            source "$project_config"
        fi
    fi

    # Validate configuration
    _config_validate
}

# Get configuration value
# Args:
#   $1 - config key name
# Returns:
#   Config value or empty string
config_get() {
    local key="$1"
    echo "${!key:-}"
}

# Check if feature is enabled
# Args:
#   $1 - feature name (e.g., "topic_extraction", "resume", "tracking")
# Returns:
#   0 if enabled, 1 if disabled
config_is_feature_enabled() {
    local feature="$1"
    local key="FEATURE_${feature^^}"  # Convert to uppercase
    local value="${!key:-false}"

    if [ "$value" = "true" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Internal: Validate configuration values
# Checks for common misconfigurations and invalid values
_config_validate() {
    # Validate LOG_LEVEL
    local level="${LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
    if [[ ! "$level" =~ ^(debug|info|warn|error)$ ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: Invalid LOG_LEVEL '${level}', defaulting to 'info'" >&2
        LOG_LEVEL="info"
    fi

    # Validate numeric values are positive integers
    local numeric_vars=(
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH"
        "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW"
        "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_ACTIVE"
        "SLEEPER_INTERVAL_IDLE"
        "SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION"
        "SLEEPER_CLARITY_EXIT"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE"
        "SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL"
        "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY"
        "STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD"
        "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE"
        "CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT"
        "CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS"
    )

    for var in "${numeric_vars[@]}"; do
        local value="${!var:-0}"
        if ! [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "WARNING: Invalid ${var} '${value}', must be positive integer" >&2
            return 1
        fi
    done

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PATH RESOLUTION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Detect execution scope (user or project)
# Determines if running from ~/.claude or project .claude
# Returns:
#   "user" or "project"
path_detect_scope() {
    local script_path
    script_path=$(readlink -f "${BASH_SOURCE[1]}")

    if [[ "$script_path" == "${HOME}/.claude"* ]]; then
        echo "user"
    else
        echo "project"
    fi
}

# Get sidekick installation directory
# Returns absolute path to sidekick root (contains sidekick.sh)
# Globals:
#   _SIDEKICK_ROOT - cached value
path_get_sidekick_root() {
    # Return cached value if available
    if [ -n "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
        echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
        return 0
    fi

    # Determine root from this script's location
    # This file is at: {root}/lib/common.sh
    local script_dir
    script_dir=$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
    _SIDEKICK_ROOT=$(cd "${script_dir}/.." && pwd)

    echo "$_SIDEKICK_ROOT"
}

# Get session-specific directory path (does NOT create directory)
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id (UUID format)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to session directory: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/{session-id}/
# Notes:
#   - CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set (Sidekick requires project context)
#   - Session state is always stored at project root in .sidekick/sessions/
#   - This directory is gitignored and contains ephemeral session state
#   - This function does NOT create the directory - use util_create_session_dir() for that
path_get_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Require CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR - Sidekick only works in project contexts
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_error "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is not set. Sidekick requires a project context."
        return 1
    fi

    # Return project-local session directory path
    echo "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}"
}

# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON input (optional, reads from stdin if empty)
# Returns:
#   Absolute path to project directory
path_get_project_dir() {
    local json="${1:-}"

    # If JSON provided, try to extract from it
    if [ -n "$json" ]; then
        local from_json
        from_json=$(json_get "$json" ".workspace.project_dir // .workspace.current_dir // empty")
        if [ -n "$from_json" ]; then
            _path_normalize "$from_json"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR environment variable
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        _path_normalize "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
        return 0
    fi

    # Fall back to current directory
    pwd
}

# Internal: Normalize path (resolve symlinks, remove trailing slash)
# Args:
#   $1 - path to normalize
# Returns:
#   Normalized absolute path
_path_normalize() {
    local path="$1"

    # Resolve to absolute path
    if [ -e "$path" ]; then
        readlink -f "$path"
    else
        # Path doesn't exist, just clean it up
        echo "$path" | sed 's:/*$::'
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# JSON PROCESSING NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Extract value from JSON using jq
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
#   $2 - jq path (e.g., ".session_id", ".workspace.project_dir")
# Returns:
#   Extracted value or empty string
json_get() {
    local json="$1"
    local path="$2"

    echo "$json" | jq -r "${path} // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""
}

# Extract session_id from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Session ID or empty string
json_get_session_id() {
    json_get "$1" ".session_id"
}

# Extract transcript_path from JSON
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   Transcript path or empty string
json_get_transcript_path() {
    json_get "$1" ".transcript_path"
}

# Validate JSON syntax
# Args:
#   $1 - JSON string
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
json_validate() {
    local json="$1"
    echo "$json" | jq empty 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract JSON from markdown code block
# Handles Claude output that wraps JSON in ```json...```
# Args:
#   $1 - text that may contain markdown-wrapped JSON
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON or original text if not wrapped
json_extract_from_markdown() {
    local text="$1"

    # Check if wrapped in markdown code block
    if echo "$text" | grep -q '^```json'; then
        # Extract content between ```json and ```
        echo "$text" | sed -n '/^```json$/,/^```$/p' | sed '1d;$d'
    else
        # Return as-is
        echo "$text"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# PROCESS MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Launch background process with PID tracking
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - process name (e.g., "sleeper", "cleanup", "analysis")
#   $3 - function name to execute
#   $@ - additional arguments to pass to function
# Creates:
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.pid - PID file
#   ${session_dir}/${name}.log - Log file
process_launch_background() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local name="$2"
    local function="$3"
    shift 3
    local args=("$@")

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/${name}.pid"
    local log_file="${session_dir}/${name}.log"

    # Check if already running
    if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
        log_debug "Process ${name} already running for session ${session_id}"
        return 0
    fi

    # Launch background process
    log_debug "Launching ${name} process for session ${session_id}"

    # Use nohup to detach from terminal and redirect output to log
    nohup bash -c "
        # Source common.sh to have access to all functions
        source '$(path_get_sidekick_root)/lib/common.sh'

        # Call the function with args
        $function ${args[*]} >> '$log_file' 2>&1
    " &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"

    log_debug "Launched ${name} with PID ${pid}"
}

# Check if process is running by PID file
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
# Returns:
#   0 if running, 1 if not running
process_is_running() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Check if PID exists and is running
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
# Args:
#   $1 - PID file path
process_kill() {
    local pid_file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    local pid
    pid=$(cat "$pid_file")

    # Try graceful shutdown first
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_debug "Sending SIGTERM to PID ${pid}"
        kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true

        # Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful shutdown
        local waited=0
        while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && [ $waited -lt 5 ]; do
            sleep 1
            waited=$((waited + 1))
        done

        # Force kill if still running
        if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Process ${pid} did not respond to SIGTERM, sending SIGKILL"
            kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
        fi
    fi

    # Remove PID file
    rm -f "$pid_file"
}

# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
# Args:
#   $1 - session directory path
process_cleanup_stale_pids() {
    local session_dir="$1"

    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        return 0
    fi

    # Find all .pid files
    local pid_file
    while IFS= read -r pid_file; do
        if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
            if ! process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
                log_debug "Cleaning up stale PID file: ${pid_file}"
                rm -f "$pid_file"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(find "$session_dir" -name "*.pid" 2>/dev/null)
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(claude_find_bin)

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Change to workspace directory and invoke Claude using project settings
    # This prevents hook recursion by using isolated .claude/settings.json
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(claude_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "Claude output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - Claude output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
claude_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
# Args:
#   $1 - identifier (e.g., "claude-invoke-12345")
# Returns:
#   Path to workspace directory
workspace_create() {
    local identifier="$1"
    local workspace="/tmp/sidekick-${identifier}"

    # Create workspace directory with .claude subdirectory
    mkdir -p "${workspace}/.claude"

    # Create isolated settings.json with hooks disabled
    # This prevents hook recursion when Claude is invoked from within a hook
    cat > "${workspace}/.claude/settings.json" <<'EOF'
{
  "hooks": {},
  "statusline": {
    "enabled": false
  }
}
EOF

    log_debug "Created isolated workspace: ${workspace}"
    echo "$workspace"
}

# Cleanup workspace
# Args:
#   $1 - workspace path
workspace_cleanup() {
    local workspace="$1"

    if [ -d "$workspace" ]; then
        log_debug "Cleaning up workspace: ${workspace}"
        rm -rf "$workspace"
    fi
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITIES NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Validate that value is non-negative integer
# Args:
#   $1 - value to validate
# Returns:
#   0 if valid, 1 if invalid
util_validate_count() {
    local value="$1"

    if [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        return 1
    fi
}

# Get file size (cross-platform)
# Args:
#   $1 - file path
# Returns:
#   File size in bytes
util_get_file_size() {
    local file="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Try stat (GNU/Linux)
    if stat --version &>/dev/null; then
        stat -c %s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    else
        # Try BSD stat (macOS)
        stat -f %z "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0"
    fi
}

# Create session directory (and parent directories) if missing
# Args:
#   $1 - session_id
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
util_create_session_dir() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || return 1

    # Create directory if it doesn't exist
    if [ ! -d "$session_dir" ]; then
        mkdir -p "$session_dir" || {
            log_error "Failed to create session directory: $session_dir"
            return 1
        }
    fi

    return 0
}

# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
# Uses approximation: 1 token ≈ 4 characters for English text
# Args:
#   $1 - transcript file path
# Returns:
#   Estimated token count
util_calculate_tokens() {
    local transcript="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Count characters and divide by 4
    local chars
    chars=$(wc -c < "$transcript" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
    echo $((chars / 4))
}

# ============================================================================
# PLUGIN LOADER NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================

# Array to track loaded plugins (feature names)
declare -a _LOADED_PLUGINS=()

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_extract_depends - Extract PLUGIN_DEPENDS declaration from a plugin file
#
# Reads the plugin file and extracts the PLUGIN_DEPENDS variable value without
# sourcing the entire file. Handles both readonly and regular variable declarations.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - plugin file path
#
# Returns:
#   Prints space-separated dependency list to stdout (empty if no dependencies)
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_extract_depends() {
    local plugin_file="$1"

    # Look for: readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="..." or PLUGIN_DEPENDS="..."
    # Extract the quoted value
    local depends
    depends=$(grep -E '^\s*(readonly\s+)?PLUGIN_DEPENDS=' "$plugin_file" 2>/dev/null | \
        sed -E 's/^\s*(readonly\s+)?PLUGIN_DEPENDS="([^"]*)".*/\2/' | \
        head -1)

    echo "$depends"
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_toposort - Topological sort of plugins based on dependencies
#
# Performs Kahn's algorithm to order plugins such that dependencies load first.
# Detects circular dependencies and reports them as errors.
#
# Arguments:
#   Takes associative array names as parameters:
#   $1 - name of associative array: plugin_name -> "dep1 dep2 ..."
#   $2 - name of array to populate with sorted plugin names
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 if circular dependency detected
#   Populates output array with sorted plugin names
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_toposort() {
    local -n deps_map="$1"
    local -n sorted_output="$2"

    # Build in-degree map and adjacency list
    declare -A in_degree
    declare -A adjacency

    # Initialize in-degree for all nodes
    for plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
        in_degree["$plugin"]=0
    done

    # Build graph and calculate in-degrees
    for plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
        local depends="${deps_map[$plugin]}"

        if [ -n "$depends" ]; then
            # Split dependencies by space
            for dep in $depends; do
                # Normalize dependency name (hyphens to underscores for comparison)
                local normalized_dep="${dep//-/_}"

                # Check if dependency exists in available plugins
                local dep_exists=false
                for available_plugin in "${!deps_map[@]}"; do
                    local normalized_available="${available_plugin//-/_}"
                    if [ "$normalized_available" = "$normalized_dep" ]; then
                        dep_exists=true
                        # Add edge: dep -> plugin
                        if [ -z "${adjacency[$available_plugin]:-}" ]; then
                            adjacency["$available_plugin"]="$plugin"
                        else
                            adjacency["$available_plugin"]="${adjacency[$available_plugin]} $plugin"
                        fi
                        # Increment in-degree
                        in_degree["$plugin"]=$((in_degree["$plugin"] + 1))
                        break
                    fi
                done

                if [ "$dep_exists" = false ]; then
                    log_error "Plugin '$plugin' depends on '$dep' which is not available or enabled"
                    return 1
                fi
            done
        fi
    done

    # Kahn's algorithm: find all nodes with in-degree 0
    local queue=()
    for plugin in "${!in_degree[@]}"; do
        if [ "${in_degree[$plugin]}" -eq 0 ]; then
            queue+=("$plugin")
        fi
    done

    # Process queue
    sorted_output=()
    while [ ${#queue[@]} -gt 0 ]; do
        # Dequeue
        local current="${queue[0]}"
        queue=("${queue[@]:1}")

        sorted_output+=("$current")

        # For each neighbor, decrease in-degree
        if [ -n "${adjacency[$current]:-}" ]; then
            for neighbor in ${adjacency[$current]}; do
                in_degree["$neighbor"]=$((in_degree["$neighbor"] - 1))
                if [ "${in_degree[$neighbor]}" -eq 0 ]; then
                    queue+=("$neighbor")
                fi
            done
        fi
    done

    # Check if all nodes were processed (no cycles)
    if [ ${#sorted_output[@]} -ne ${#deps_map[@]} ]; then
        log_error "Circular dependency detected in plugins"

        # Find which plugins are in the cycle
        for plugin in "${!in_degree[@]}"; do
            if [ "${in_degree[$plugin]}" -gt 0 ]; then
                log_error "  Plugin '$plugin' is part of dependency cycle (in-degree: ${in_degree[$plugin]})"
            fi
        done

        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_discover_and_load - Discover and source all enabled feature plugins
#
# Scans the features/ directory, sources enabled features in dependency order.
# Plugins declare dependencies via PLUGIN_DEPENDS="dep1 dep2" variable.
#
# Globals:
#   _LOADED_PLUGINS - populated with loaded feature names
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (even if some plugins fail to load)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_discover_and_load() {
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local features_dir="$sidekick_root/features"

    # Reset loaded plugins array
    _LOADED_PLUGINS=()

    # Check if features directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$features_dir" ]; then
        log_warn "Features directory not found: $features_dir"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Discovering plugins in: $features_dir"

    # Find all feature files (sorted alphabetically)
    local feature_files=()
    while IFS= read -r -d '' feature_file; do
        feature_files+=("$feature_file")
    done < <(find "$features_dir" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.sh" -type f -print0 | sort -z)

    # Load each enabled feature
    for feature_file in "${feature_files[@]}"; do
        local feature_name
        feature_name=$(basename "$feature_file" .sh)

        # Normalize feature name for config lookup (replace hyphens with underscores)
        local config_name="${feature_name//-/_}"

        # Check if feature is enabled in config
        if config_is_feature_enabled "$config_name"; then
            log_debug "Loading plugin: $feature_name from $feature_file"

            # Source the feature file
            # shellcheck disable=SC1090
            if source "$feature_file"; then
                _LOADED_PLUGINS+=("$feature_name")
                log_debug "Plugin loaded successfully: $feature_name"
            else
                log_error "Failed to source plugin: $feature_name (continuing with other plugins)"
            fi
        else
            log_debug "Skipping disabled plugin: $feature_name"
        fi
    done

    log_info "Loaded ${#_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]} plugin(s): ${_LOADED_PLUGINS[*]}"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# plugin_invoke_hook - Invoke a hook on all loaded plugins
#
# Calls {feature}_{hook_name}() for each loaded plugin if the function exists.
# Collects stdout from all hooks and returns concatenated output.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - hook_name (e.g., "on_session_start", "on_user_prompt_submit")
#   $@ - remaining args passed to each hook function
#
# Returns:
#   Concatenated stdout from all hooks (empty if no output)
#   Exit code: 0 (hook failures are logged but non-fatal)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
plugin_invoke_hook() {
    local hook_name="$1"
    shift
    local args=("$@")

    local aggregated_output=""

    log_debug "Invoking hook '$hook_name' on ${#_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]} plugin(s)"

    for plugin in "${_LOADED_PLUGINS[@]}"; do
        # Normalize plugin name for function lookup (replace hyphens with underscores)
        local normalized_plugin="${plugin//-/_}"
        local hook_function="${normalized_plugin}_${hook_name}"

        # Check if hook function exists
        if declare -f "$hook_function" &>/dev/null; then
            log_debug "Calling hook: $hook_function"

            # Call hook and capture output
            local output=""
            if output=$("$hook_function" "${args[@]}" 2>&1); then
                # Append non-empty output
                if [ -n "$output" ]; then
                    if [ -n "$aggregated_output" ]; then
                        # Multiple outputs - concatenate with newline
                        aggregated_output="${aggregated_output}"$'\n'"${output}"
                    else
                        aggregated_output="$output"
                    fi
                fi
            else
                log_warn "Hook $hook_function failed with exit code $? (non-fatal, continuing)"
            fi
        else
            log_debug "Hook $hook_function not defined (skipping)"
        fi
    done

    # Output aggregated result
    echo "$aggregated_output"
    return 0
}

input.old_string:
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_check_reminder - Check if static reminder is due
#
# Checks if we should output a static reminder based on the counter value
# and configured cadence. Loads static reminder text from files.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - count (current response count)
#   $2 - project_dir (optional, for project-specific reminders)
#
# Returns:
#   Prints reminder text to stdout if due, empty if not
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_check_reminder() {
    local count="$1"
    local project_dir="${2:-}"

    # Get cadence from config (default to 4)
    local cadence
    cadence=$(config_get "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE")
    [ -z "$cadence" ] && cadence=4

    # Check if reminder is due (count is multiple of cadence, but not 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] && [ $((count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "Static reminder due at count $count (cadence $cadence)"

        # Try to load reminder text from files (project overrides user)
        local reminder_text=""

        # Try project-specific reminder first
        if [ -n "$project_dir" ]; then
            local project_reminder="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$project_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$project_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded project reminder from $project_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Fall back to user reminder if no project reminder
        if [ -z "$reminder_text" ]; then
            local sidekick_root
            sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
            local user_reminder="$sidekick_root/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$user_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$user_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded user reminder from $user_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Output reminder if we have one
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            echo "$reminder_text"
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder file found"
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for tracking feature
#
# Increments counter and outputs reminder JSON if cadence is met
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   Outputs hook JSON to stdout if reminder is due
#   Exit code: 0 (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Tracking: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Increment counter
    local count
    count=$(tracking_increment "$session_id")
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        log_error "Failed to increment tracking counter"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Current count: $count"

    # Check if static reminder is due
    local reminder_text
    reminder_text=$(tracking_check_reminder "$count" "$project_dir")

    if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
        log_info "Static reminder due at count $count"

        # Escape reminder text for JSON (replace newlines with \n, escape quotes)
        local escaped_reminder
        escaped_reminder=$(echo "$reminder_text" | jq -Rs .)

        # Output hook JSON with additionalContext
        cat <<JSON
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
    "additionalContext": $escaped_reminder
  }
}
JSON
    else
        log_debug "No static reminder due at count $count"
    fi

    return 0
}

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Tracking
# Request counting and static reminder injection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before tracking.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_init - Initialize tracking counter for a session
#
# Creates the response_count file and initializes it to 0
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_init() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_init: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Initialize counter to 0
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking initialized: $counter_file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_increment - Increment counter and return new value
#
# Atomically increments the counter and returns the new value
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the new counter value to stdout
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_increment() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_increment: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        log_warn "Counter file not found, initializing: $counter_file"
        tracking_init "$session_id" || return 1
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    # Increment
    local new_value=$((current + 1))

    # Write back
    echo "$new_value" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking incremented: $current -> $new_value"

    # Output new value
    echo "$new_value"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_get - Get current counter value
#
# Reads and returns the current counter value without incrementing
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the current counter value to stdout (0 if not initialized)
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_get() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_get: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$current"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_check_reminder - Check if static reminder is due
#
# Checks if we should output a static reminder based on the counter value
# and configured cadence. Loads static reminder text from files.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - count (current response count)
#   $2 - project_dir (optional, for project-specific reminders)
#
# Returns:
#   Prints reminder text to stdout if due, empty if not
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_check_reminder() {
    local count="$1"
    local project_dir="${2:-}"

    # Get cadence from config (default to 4)
    local cadence
    cadence=$(config_get "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE")
    [ -z "$cadence" ] && cadence=4

    # Check if reminder is due (count is multiple of cadence, but not 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] && [ $((count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "Static reminder due at count $count (cadence $cadence)"

        # Try to load reminder text from files (project overrides user)
        local reminder_text=""

        # Try project-specific reminder first
        if [ -n "$project_dir" ]; then
            local project_reminder="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$project_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$project_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded project reminder from $project_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Fall back to user reminder if no project reminder
        if [ -z "$reminder_text" ]; then
            local sidekick_root
            sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
            local user_reminder="$sidekick_root/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$user_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$user_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded user reminder from $user_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Output reminder if we have one
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            echo "$reminder_text"
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder file found"
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_on_session_start - SessionStart hook for tracking feature
#
# Initializes the tracking counter for the new session
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_on_session_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    log_debug "Tracking: SessionStart hook"

    if ! tracking_init "$session_id"; then
        log_warn "Failed to initialize tracking (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for tracking feature
#
# Increments counter and outputs reminder JSON if cadence is met
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   Outputs hook JSON to stdout if reminder is due
#   Exit code: 0 (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Tracking: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Increment counter
    local count
    count=$(tracking_increment "$session_id")
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        log_error "Failed to increment tracking counter"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Current count: $count"

    # Check if static reminder is due
    local reminder_text
    reminder_text=$(tracking_check_reminder "$count" "$project_dir")

    if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
        log_info "Static reminder due at count $count"

        # Escape reminder text for JSON (replace newlines with \n, escape quotes)
        local escaped_reminder
        escaped_reminder=$(echo "$reminder_text" | jq -Rs .)

        # Output hook JSON with additionalContext
        cat <<JSON
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
    "additionalContext": $escaped_reminder
  }
}
JSON
    else
        log_debug "No static reminder due at count $count"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Tracking feature loaded"

#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Tracking
# Request counting and static reminder injection

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_TRACKING_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before tracking.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_init - Initialize tracking counter for a session
#
# Creates the response_count file and initializes it to 0
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_init() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_init: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Initialize counter to 0
    echo "0" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking initialized: $counter_file"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_increment - Increment counter and return new value
#
# Atomically increments the counter and returns the new value
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the new counter value to stdout
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_increment() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_increment: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        log_warn "Counter file not found, initializing: $counter_file"
        tracking_init "$session_id" || return 1
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    # Increment
    local new_value=$((current + 1))

    # Write back
    echo "$new_value" > "$counter_file"

    log_debug "Tracking incremented: $current -> $new_value"

    # Output new value
    echo "$new_value"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_get - Get current counter value
#
# Reads and returns the current counter value without incrementing
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#
# Returns:
#   Prints the current counter value to stdout (0 if not initialized)
#   Exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_get() {
    local session_id="$1"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ]; then
        log_error "tracking_get: session_id is required"
        return 1
    fi

    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local counter_file="$session_dir/response_count"

    # Check if counter file exists
    if [ ! -f "$counter_file" ]; then
        echo "0"
        return 0
    fi

    # Read current value
    local current
    current=$(cat "$counter_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    # Validate it's a number
    if ! util_validate_count "$current"; then
        log_error "Invalid counter value in $counter_file: $current"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$current"
    return 0
}


#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_on_session_start - SessionStart hook for tracking feature
#
# Initializes the tracking counter for the new session
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_on_session_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    log_debug "Tracking: SessionStart hook"

    if ! tracking_init "$session_id"; then
        log_warn "Failed to initialize tracking (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# tracking_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for tracking feature
#
# Increments counter and outputs reminder JSON if cadence is met
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   Outputs hook JSON to stdout if reminder is due
#   Exit code: 0 (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
tracking_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Tracking: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Increment counter
    local count
    count=$(tracking_increment "$session_id")
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        log_error "Failed to increment tracking counter"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Current count: $count"

    # Check if static reminder is due
    local reminder_text
    reminder_text=$(tracking_check_reminder "$count" "$project_dir")

    if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
        log_info "Static reminder due at count $count"

        # Escape reminder text for JSON (replace newlines with \n, escape quotes)
        local escaped_reminder
        escaped_reminder=$(echo "$reminder_text" | jq -Rs .)

        # Output hook JSON with additionalContext
        cat <<JSON
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
    "additionalContext": $escaped_reminder
  }
}
JSON
    else
        log_debug "No static reminder due at count $count"
    fi

    return 0
}

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Reminder
# Periodic static reminder injection based on response count

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_REMINDER_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_REMINDER_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before reminder.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Declare dependency on tracking feature
readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="tracking"

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# reminder_check_due - Check if static reminder is due
#
# Checks if we should output a static reminder based on the counter value
# and configured cadence. Loads static reminder text from files.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - count (current response count)
#   $2 - project_dir (optional, for project-specific reminders)
#
# Returns:
#   Prints reminder text to stdout if due, empty if not
#   Exit code: 0 always
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
reminder_check_due() {
    local count="$1"
    local project_dir="${2:-}"

    # Get cadence from config (default to 4)
    local cadence
    cadence=$(config_get "TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE")
    [ -z "$cadence" ] && cadence=4

    # Check if reminder is due (count is multiple of cadence, but not 0)
    if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] && [ $((count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_debug "Static reminder due at count $count (cadence $cadence)"

        # Try to load reminder text from files (project overrides user)
        local reminder_text=""

        # Try project-specific reminder first
        if [ -n "$project_dir" ]; then
            local project_reminder="$project_dir/.claude/hooks/sidekick/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$project_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$project_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded project reminder from $project_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Fall back to user reminder if no project reminder
        if [ -z "$reminder_text" ]; then
            local sidekick_root
            sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
            local user_reminder="$sidekick_root/static-reminder.txt"
            if [ -f "$user_reminder" ]; then
                reminder_text=$(cat "$user_reminder")
                log_debug "Loaded user reminder from $user_reminder"
            fi
        fi

        # Output reminder if we have one
        if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
            echo "$reminder_text"
        else
            log_debug "No static reminder file found"
        fi
    fi

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# reminder_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for reminder feature
#
# Reads current count from tracking feature and outputs reminder JSON if cadence is met
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   Outputs hook JSON to stdout if reminder is due
#   Exit code: 0 (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
reminder_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Reminder: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Get current count from tracking feature (tracking already incremented it)
    local count
    count=$(tracking_get "$session_id")
    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
        log_error "Failed to get tracking counter"
        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Current count from tracking: $count"

    # Check if static reminder is due
    local reminder_text
    reminder_text=$(reminder_check_due "$count" "$project_dir")

    if [ -n "$reminder_text" ]; then
        log_info "Static reminder due at count $count"

        # Escape reminder text for JSON (replace newlines with \n, escape quotes)
        local escaped_reminder
        escaped_reminder=$(echo "$reminder_text" | jq -Rs .)

        # Output hook JSON with additionalContext
        cat <<JSON
{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
    "additionalContext": $escaped_reminder
  }
}
JSON
    else
        log_debug "No static reminder due at count $count"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Reminder feature loaded"

# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting and static reminders
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku

# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80

# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
RESUME_MODEL=haiku

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

content.text: Now let me create unit tests for the dependency resolution system:

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Unit tests for plugin dependency resolution

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"

# Source test utilities
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/../lib/test-utils.sh"

# Source the library under test
source "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh"

# ============================================================================
# TEST: plugin_extract_depends - Extract dependencies from plugin file
# ============================================================================

test_extract_depends_no_deps() {
    local test_file="/tmp/test-plugin-no-deps.sh"

    cat > "$test_file" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Test plugin without dependencies

test_function() {
    echo "test"
}
EOF

    local result
    result=$(plugin_extract_depends "$test_file")

    assert_equals "" "$result" "Should return empty string for plugin without PLUGIN_DEPENDS"

    rm -f "$test_file"
}

test_extract_depends_with_deps() {
    local test_file="/tmp/test-plugin-with-deps.sh"

    cat > "$test_file" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
# Test plugin with dependencies

readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="tracking statusline"

test_function() {
    echo "test"
}
EOF

    local result
    result=$(plugin_extract_depends "$test_file")

    assert_equals "tracking statusline" "$result" "Should extract dependency list"

    rm -f "$test_file"
}

test_extract_depends_readonly() {
    local test_file="/tmp/test-plugin-readonly.sh"

    cat > "$test_file" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="foo bar"
EOF

    local result
    result=$(plugin_extract_depends "$test_file")

    assert_equals "foo bar" "$result" "Should extract readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS"

    rm -f "$test_file"
}

# ============================================================================
# TEST: plugin_toposort - Topological sort of plugins
# ============================================================================

test_toposort_no_deps() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-b"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-c"]=""

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 3 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 3 plugins in sorted output"

    # All plugins should be present (order doesn't matter without dependencies)
    local found_a=false found_b=false found_c=false
    for plugin in "${sorted[@]}"; do
        case "$plugin" in
            plugin-a) found_a=true ;;
            plugin-b) found_b=true ;;
            plugin-c) found_c=true ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "$found_a" "plugin-a should be in sorted output"
    assert_true "$found_b" "plugin-b should be in sorted output"
    assert_true "$found_c" "plugin-c should be in sorted output"
}

test_toposort_simple_chain() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-b"]="plugin-a"
    deps_map["plugin-c"]="plugin-b"

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 3 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 3 plugins in sorted output"

    # Check order: a should come before b, b before c
    local idx_a=-1 idx_b=-1 idx_c=-1
    for i in "${!sorted[@]}"; do
        case "${sorted[$i]}" in
            plugin-a) idx_a=$i ;;
            plugin-b) idx_b=$i ;;
            plugin-c) idx_c=$i ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "[ $idx_a -lt $idx_b ]" "plugin-a should come before plugin-b"
    assert_true "[ $idx_b -lt $idx_c ]" "plugin-b should come before plugin-c"
}

test_toposort_diamond() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["base"]=""
    deps_map["left"]="base"
    deps_map["right"]="base"
    deps_map["top"]="left right"

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 4 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 4 plugins in sorted output"

    # Check order: base should come before left and right, left and right before top
    local idx_base=-1 idx_left=-1 idx_right=-1 idx_top=-1
    for i in "${!sorted[@]}"; do
        case "${sorted[$i]}" in
            base) idx_base=$i ;;
            left) idx_left=$i ;;
            right) idx_right=$i ;;
            top) idx_top=$i ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "[ $idx_base -lt $idx_left ]" "base should come before left"
    assert_true "[ $idx_base -lt $idx_right ]" "base should come before right"
    assert_true "[ $idx_left -lt $idx_top ]" "left should come before top"
    assert_true "[ $idx_right -lt $idx_top ]" "right should come before top"
}

test_toposort_circular_dependency() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]="plugin-b"
    deps_map["plugin-b"]="plugin-c"
    deps_map["plugin-c"]="plugin-a"

    local sorted=()

    # Should fail with circular dependency
    if plugin_toposort deps_map sorted 2>/dev/null; then
        fail "Should detect circular dependency"
    else
        pass "Detected circular dependency"
    fi
}

test_toposort_missing_dependency() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-b"]="plugin-nonexistent"

    local sorted=()

    # Should fail with missing dependency
    if plugin_toposort deps_map sorted 2>/dev/null; then
        fail "Should detect missing dependency"
    else
        pass "Detected missing dependency"
    fi
}

test_toposort_hyphen_normalization() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["topic-extraction"]=""
    deps_map["reminder"]="topic_extraction"  # Reference with underscore

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 2 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 2 plugins in sorted output"

    # topic-extraction should come before reminder
    local idx_topic=-1 idx_reminder=-1
    for i in "${!sorted[@]}"; do
        case "${sorted[$i]}" in
            topic-extraction) idx_topic=$i ;;
            reminder) idx_reminder=$i ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "[ $idx_topic -lt $idx_reminder ]" "topic-extraction should come before reminder"
}

# ============================================================================
# RUN TESTS
# ============================================================================

# Setup
test_setup() {
    # Set required environment variables for common.sh
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="/tmp/test-project"
    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.sidekick/sessions"
}

# Teardown
test_teardown() {
    rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
}

# Run all tests
run_test_suite() {
    echo "========================================"
    echo "Unit Tests: Plugin Dependencies"
    echo "========================================"

    test_setup

    # Extract dependencies tests
    run_test "plugin_extract_depends: no dependencies" test_extract_depends_no_deps
    run_test "plugin_extract_depends: with dependencies" test_extract_depends_with_deps
    run_test "plugin_extract_depends: readonly declaration" test_extract_depends_readonly

    # Topological sort tests
    run_test "plugin_toposort: no dependencies" test_toposort_no_deps
    run_test "plugin_toposort: simple chain" test_toposort_simple_chain
    run_test "plugin_toposort: diamond pattern" test_toposort_diamond
    run_test "plugin_toposort: circular dependency" test_toposort_circular_dependency
    run_test "plugin_toposort: missing dependency" test_toposort_missing_dependency
    run_test "plugin_toposort: hyphen normalization" test_toposort_hyphen_normalization

    test_teardown

    print_test_summary
}

run_test_suite

    local result
    result=$(plugin_extract_depends "$test_file")

    assert_equals "tracking statusline" "$result" "Should extract dependency list"

    rm -f "$test_file"
}

test_extract_depends_readonly() {
    local test_file="/tmp/test-plugin-readonly.sh"

    cat > "$test_file" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS="foo bar"
EOF

    local result
    result=$(plugin_extract_depends "$test_file")

    assert_equals "foo bar" "$result" "Should extract readonly PLUGIN_DEPENDS"

    rm -f "$test_file"
}

# ============================================================================
# TEST: plugin_toposort - Topological sort of plugins
# ============================================================================

test_toposort_no_deps() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-b"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-c"]=""

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 3 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 3 plugins in sorted output"

    # All plugins should be present (order doesn't matter without dependencies)
    local found_a=false found_b=false found_c=false
    for plugin in "${sorted[@]}"; do
        case "$plugin" in
            plugin-a) found_a=true ;;
            plugin-b) found_b=true ;;
            plugin-c) found_c=true ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "$found_a" "plugin-a should be in sorted output"
    assert_true "$found_b" "plugin-b should be in sorted output"
    assert_true "$found_c" "plugin-c should be in sorted output"
}

test_toposort_simple_chain() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-b"]="plugin-a"
    deps_map["plugin-c"]="plugin-b"

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 3 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 3 plugins in sorted output"

    # Check order: a should come before b, b before c
    local idx_a=-1 idx_b=-1 idx_c=-1
    for i in "${!sorted[@]}"; do
        case "${sorted[$i]}" in
            plugin-a) idx_a=$i ;;
            plugin-b) idx_b=$i ;;
            plugin-c) idx_c=$i ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "[ $idx_a -lt $idx_b ]" "plugin-a should come before plugin-b"
    assert_true "[ $idx_b -lt $idx_c ]" "plugin-b should come before plugin-c"
}

test_toposort_diamond() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["base"]=""
    deps_map["left"]="base"
    deps_map["right"]="base"
    deps_map["top"]="left right"

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 4 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 4 plugins in sorted output"

    # Check order: base should come before left and right, left and right before top
    local idx_base=-1 idx_left=-1 idx_right=-1 idx_top=-1
    for i in "${!sorted[@]}"; do
        case "${sorted[$i]}" in
            base) idx_base=$i ;;
            left) idx_left=$i ;;
            right) idx_right=$i ;;
            top) idx_top=$i ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "[ $idx_base -lt $idx_left ]" "base should come before left"
    assert_true "[ $idx_base -lt $idx_right ]" "base should come before right"
    assert_true "[ $idx_left -lt $idx_top ]" "left should come before top"
    assert_true "[ $idx_right -lt $idx_top ]" "right should come before top"
}

test_toposort_circular_dependency() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]="plugin-b"
    deps_map["plugin-b"]="plugin-c"
    deps_map["plugin-c"]="plugin-a"

    local sorted=()

    # Should fail with circular dependency
    if plugin_toposort deps_map sorted 2>/dev/null; then
        fail "Should detect circular dependency"
    else
        pass "Detected circular dependency"
    fi
}

test_toposort_missing_dependency() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["plugin-a"]=""
    deps_map["plugin-b"]="plugin-nonexistent"

    local sorted=()

    # Should fail with missing dependency
    if plugin_toposort deps_map sorted 2>/dev/null; then
        fail "Should detect missing dependency"
    else
        pass "Detected missing dependency"
    fi
}

test_toposort_hyphen_normalization() {
    declare -A deps_map
    deps_map["topic-extraction"]=""
    deps_map["reminder"]="topic_extraction"  # Reference with underscore

    local sorted=()
    plugin_toposort deps_map sorted

    assert_equals 2 "${#sorted[@]}" "Should have 2 plugins in sorted output"

    # topic-extraction should come before reminder
    local idx_topic=-1 idx_reminder=-1
    for i in "${!sorted[@]}"; do
        case "${sorted[$i]}" in
            topic-extraction) idx_topic=$i ;;
            reminder) idx_reminder=$i ;;
        esac
    done

    assert_true "[ $idx_topic -lt $idx_reminder ]" "topic-extraction should come before reminder"
}

# ============================================================================
# RUN TESTS
# ============================================================================

# Setup
test_setup() {
    # Set required environment variables for common.sh
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="/tmp/test-project"
    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.sidekick/sessions"
}

# Teardown
test_teardown() {
    rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
}

# Run all tests
run_test_suite() {
    echo "========================================"
    echo "Unit Tests: Plugin Dependencies"
    echo "========================================"

    test_setup

    # Extract dependencies tests
    run_test "plugin_extract_depends: no dependencies" test_extract_depends_no_deps
    run_test "plugin_extract_depends: with dependencies" test_extract_depends_with_deps
    run_test "plugin_extract_depends: readonly declaration" test_extract_depends_readonly

    # Topological sort tests
    run_test "plugin_toposort: no dependencies" test_toposort_no_deps
    run_test "plugin_toposort: simple chain" test_toposort_simple_chain
    run_test "plugin_toposort: diamond pattern" test_toposort_diamond
    run_test "plugin_toposort: circular dependency" test_toposort_circular_dependency
    run_test "plugin_toposort: missing dependency" test_toposort_missing_dependency
    run_test "plugin_toposort: hyphen normalization" test_toposort_hyphen_normalization

    test_teardown

    print_test_summary
}

run_test_suite

#!/bin/bash
# test-plugin-dependencies.sh - Unit tests for plugin dependency resolution
#
# Tests the PLUGIN LOADER namespace from lib/common.sh

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0

# Get script directory and project root
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
    export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR/project"
    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.sidekick/sessions"

    # Source the library under test
    source "$PROJECT_ROOT/src/sidekick/lib/common.sh"
}

# Teardown
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}

# Test assertion helpers
assert_equals() {
    local expected="$1"
    local actual="$2"
    local message="${3:-}"

    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
        return 0
    else
        echo "  Expected: '$expected'"
        echo "  Actual:   '$actual'"
        if [ -n "$message" ]; then
            echo "  Message:  $message"
        fi
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_true() {
    local condition="$1"
    local message="${2:-}"

    if eval "$condition"; then
        return 0
    else
        echo "  Condition failed: $condition"
        if [ -n "$message" ]; then
            echo "  Message: $message"
        fi
        return 1
    fi
}

# Test runner
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    local test_func="$2"

    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    echo -n "Testing: $test_name ... "

    if $test_func 2>&1 | grep -q "^"; then
        local output=$($test_func 2>&1 || true)
        if [ -z "$output" ]; then
            echo -e "${GREEN}PASS${RESET}"
            TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        else
            echo -e "${RED}FAIL${RESET}"
            echo "$output" | sed 's/^/  /'
            TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
        fi
    fi
}

# Print test summary
print_summary() {
    echo ""
    echo "========================================"
    echo "Tests run: $TESTS_RUN"
    echo -e "${GREEN}Passed: $TESTS_PASSED${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "${RED}Failed: $TESTS_FAILED${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo "All tests passed!"
        exit 0
    fi
}
